rationale: Poly- means many or much. Polydipsia means excessive thirst polyuria means excessive urination and polypharmacy means taking many medications.
Oligo- means few scanty or little. Oliguria means decreased urine output. It is the opposite of poly- which means many.
Eu- means normal or good. Eupnea means normal breathing and euthyroid means normal thyroid function. It indicates a healthy or normal state.

definition: Prefix meaning difficult painful or abnormal
rationale: Dys- means difficult painful or abnormal. Dyspnea means difficulty breathing dysrhythmia means abnormal rhythm and dysphagia means difficulty swallowing.
Mal- means bad or poor. Malaise means general discomfort malignant means cancerous or harmful and malnutrition means poor nutrition.
Pseudo- means false or fake. Pseudoseizure is a non-epileptic event resembling a seizure. The prefix indicates something that appears to be something it is not.
Ante- means before. Antepartum means before birth and anterior means toward the front or before. It indicates something that comes first.
Post- means after. Postpartum means after birth postictal means after a seizure and posterior means toward the back or behind.
Peri- means around or surrounding. Pericardium is the membrane around the heart and peripheral means around the edges or outer areas.
Trans- means across or through. Transdermal means through the skin transport means to carry across and transfusion moves blood from one person to another.
Retro- means behind or backward. Retroperitoneal means behind the peritoneum and retrograde means moving backward.
Contra- means against or opposite. Contraindication is a reason not to give something and contralateral means the opposite side.
Ipsi- means same side. Ipsilateral means on the same side. If injury is on the right ipsilateral findings would also be on the right.
The suffix -ectomy means surgical removal or excision. Appendectomy is removal of appendix and cholecystectomy is removal of gallbladder.
The suffix -ostomy means creating an artificial opening. Tracheostomy is an opening in the trachea and colostomy is an opening in the colon.
The suffix -otomy means cutting into or incision. Tracheotomy is cutting into the trachea. It differs from -ostomy which creates a permanent opening.

definition: Suffix meaning surgical repair or reconstruction
rationale: The suffix -plasty means surgical repair or reconstruction. Angioplasty is repair of blood vessels and rhinoplasty is reconstruction of the nose.
The suffix -scopy means visual examination using a scope. Endoscopy is examination of the interior and bronchoscopy is examination of the bronchi.

definition: Suffix meaning a record or image
rationale: The suffix -gram means a record or image. Electrocardiogram ECG is a record of heart electrical activity and radiogram is an x-ray image.
The suffix -graph means the instrument that records. Electrocardiograph is the machine that records the ECG. Compare to -gram which is the record itself.
The suffix -meter means an instrument for measuring. Thermometer measures temperature and sphygmomanometer measures blood pressure.

definition: Suffix meaning surgical puncture to remove fluid
rationale: The suffix -centesis means surgical puncture to remove fluid. Thoracentesis removes fluid from the chest and paracentesis removes fluid from the abdomen.
The suffix -lysis means breakdown destruction or loosening. Hemolysis is breakdown of red blood cells and thrombolysis is breakdown of blood clots.
The suffix -stasis means stopping controlling or standing still. Hemostasis is stopping bleeding and homeostasis is maintaining stable internal conditions.
The suffix -phagia means eating or swallowing. Dysphagia is difficulty swallowing and polyphagia is excessive eating.
The suffix -phasia refers to speech. Aphasia is inability to speak or understand speech and dysphasia is difficulty with speech.
The suffix -plegia means paralysis. Hemiplegia is paralysis of one side paraplegia is paralysis of lower body and quadriplegia is paralysis of all four limbs.

definition: Suffix meaning weakness or partial paralysis
rationale: The suffix -paresis means weakness or partial paralysis less than complete. Hemiparesis is weakness on one side. It is less severe than -plegia.
The suffix -pnea relates to breathing. Dyspnea is difficulty breathing apnea is absence of breathing tachypnea is fast breathing and eupnea is normal breathing.
The suffix -uria relates to urine. Hematuria is blood in urine polyuria is excessive urination and oliguria is decreased urination.
Cardi/o refers to the heart. Cardiac means pertaining to the heart cardiomyopathy is heart muscle disease and tachycardia is fast heart rate.
Pulmo or pulmon refers to the lungs. Pulmonary means pertaining to the lungs and cardiopulmonary refers to heart and lungs together.
Hepat/o refers to the liver. Hepatitis is liver inflammation hepatomegaly is enlarged liver and hepatic means pertaining to the liver.
Ren/o and nephr/o both refer to the kidney. Renal means pertaining to kidneys and nephritis is kidney inflammation.
Gastr/o refers to the stomach. Gastritis is stomach inflammation gastric means pertaining to stomach and gastroenteritis is stomach and intestinal inflammation.
Enter/o refers to the small intestine. Enteritis is intestinal inflammation and gastroenteritis affects both stomach and intestines.
Cephal/o refers to the head. Cephalic means toward the head encephalitis is brain inflammation and hydrocephalus is water on the brain.
Encephal/o refers to the brain specifically. Encephalitis is brain inflammation and encephalopathy is brain disease or dysfunction.
Neur/o refers to nerves. Neurology is study of nerves neuropathy is nerve disease and neurological pertains to the nervous system.
My/o refers to muscle. Myocardium is heart muscle myalgia is muscle pain and myopathy is muscle disease.
Oste/o refers to bone. Osteoporosis is porous bones osteomyelitis is bone infection and fracture is a broken bone.
Arthr/o refers to joints. Arthritis is joint inflammation arthroscopy is joint examination and arthroplasty is joint repair.
Derm/o and dermat/o refer to skin. Dermatitis is skin inflammation dermatology is study of skin and subcutaneous means under the skin.
Hem/o and hemat/o refer to blood. Hemorrhage is bleeding hematoma is blood collection and hematology is study of blood.
Angi/o refers to blood vessels. Angiography is vessel imaging angioplasty is vessel repair and angioedema is vessel-related swelling.
Phleb/o refers to veins. Phlebitis is vein inflammation phlebotomy is vein puncture to draw blood and thrombophlebitis is clot in an inflamed vein.
Thromb/o refers to blood clots. Thrombosis is clot formation thrombus is a clot and thrombocyte is a platelet which helps clotting.

definition: Closer to the trunk or point of attachment
rationale: Proximal means closer to the trunk or point of attachment. The elbow is proximal to the wrist. It is the opposite of distal.

definition: Farther from the trunk or point of attachment
rationale: Distal means farther from the trunk or point of attachment. The fingers are distal to the elbow. It is the opposite of proximal.
Superficial means near the surface or shallow. A superficial wound affects only outer layers. It is the opposite of deep.
